# Align AGENTS.md identity anchors with ADR-0018 OD-3 two-hemisphere

## Context

Sibling leaf of the target-architecture composition ADR (graduated from Discussion #13846), split out per @neo-gpt's graduation constraint: the identity-substrate alignment must be **governed, never a stealth rider** inside an architecture PR. Live latest-open sweep at 2026-07-03T00:55:29Z: no equivalent ticket.

## The Problem

`AGENTS.md` — the always-loaded session substrate — still asserts "four co-load-bearing pillars" in TWO anchors: the top `§core_values` bridge text and `§neo_identity_anchor`. That is STALE against the operator's own decision (ADR-0018 OD-3, 2026-05-30): **Two Hemispheres is the canonical top-level scaffold** (Body `/src/` ↔ Brain `/ai/`, joined by the Neural Link); Four Pillars is demoted to Brain-internal elaboration. Every session (including this one — verified live) boots carrying an identity frame the identity authority already overruled. ADR-0018 §2 itself notes AGENTS.md "had self-contradictory pillar wording."

## The Fix

A narrow, governed edit repairing BOTH anchors to the two-hemisphere-canonical framing (four-pillar retained only as the Brain-internal elaboration it now is), executed under:
- **ADR-0018 OD-3** (the deciding authority — this is a staleness fix, not a re-decision),
- **`neo-identity-update`** (the identity-surface change protocol; AGENTS.md is one of the ~30 identity-encoding surfaces),
- **`turn-memory-pre-flight`** (AGENTS.md is loaded instruction substrate: the PR carries the slot-rationale section — disposition `rewrite` on both anchors, net byte movement stated).

## Acceptance Criteria

- [ ] Both four-pillar anchors rewritten two-hemisphere-canonical; four-pillar language survives only as explicitly Brain-internal elaboration
- [ ] PR body carries the turn-memory-pre-flight slot-rationale (per-anchor disposition + load effect) and cites ADR-0018 OD-3 as the deciding authority
- [ ] `neo-identity-update` surface checklist consulted; any OTHER surface still carrying four-pillar-as-top-level is listed (fix or follow-up per scale)
- [ ] Cross-family review before merge (identity substrate)
- [ ] PR `Resolves` this leaf

## Out of Scope

- The composition ADR itself (the sibling authoring leaf).
- Re-deciding the scaffold (ADR-0018 OD-3 already did).

## Related

Related: Discussion #13846 (constraint origin), ADR 0018 (authority), the composition-ADR authoring leaf (sibling)
